_changelog: v2 (Phase 2, 2026-07-14): added give_first / who_am_i / offer / cta_time_proposals per variant; applies Nick Saraev's 4-step + 7-principle framework end to end

voice.tone: confident, concrete, second-person, one-to-one text-message frame — never corporate. Sound like a friend who knows their business.
voice.text_message_test: would a friend reading this over your shoulder think it's personal, or a mass send? Optimize for personal.

tech_to_outcome.RAG / vector search: instant, accurate answers pulled from your own docs — no one digging through PDFs
tech_to_outcome.agents / multi-agent: work that runs itself, 24/7, without another hire
tech_to_outcome.cold-email engine: a machine that books qualified meetings while you sleep
tech_to_outcome.voice AI (Vapi/Retell): every inbound call answered and qualified — at 3am, on a Sunday
tech_to_outcome.n8n / Modal automation: 100 manual hours a week your team does by hand — gone
tech_to_outcome.<30-day ship cycle: live this month, not next quarter
tech_to_outcome.full-stack / mobile: a real product in your users' hands in weeks
tech_to_outcome.Built with Claude Code: one senior operator moving at the speed of a whole team

nick_framework_rules.structure_4_step: personalization -> who am I -> offer -> CTA (all four, no exceptions)
nick_framework_rules.psychology_7_principles: give first, micro-commitments, social proof, authority, rapport, scarcity, shared identity — hit at least 5 of 7
nick_framework_rules.offer_master_sentence: I will do X thing for you in Y time or Z risk mitigation
nick_framework_rules.cta_doctrine: one action, two specific times, minimize steps to yes

opener_constraints._max_words_reasoning: Nick's rule = personalization is ≤2 sentences, 1 ideal (video timestamp 35:22+, chunk1). Two-sentence observations run 18-25 words. Phase-1 cap of 15 was under Nick's floor — bumped to 22 to allow Nick-style two-clause openers with an em-dash pivot.
opener_constraints.must_reference: one specific fact about the prospect derived from lead.notes or research signal
opener_constraints.must_be: an observation that signals research, not a pitch
opener_constraints.casing_preference: lowercase or sentence-case; never Title Case or ALL CAPS

subject_constraints.must_be: a question or specific-outcome or curiosity hook — never a pitch

give_first_constraints.must_be_concrete: a specific observation, spotted gap, insight, credit, or sample — never 'value' or 'thoughts'
must_not_be: a request for feedback disguised as a gift
a request to jump on a call

who_am_i_constraints.must_lead_with: who I've worked with + past quantified result — NOT what I do

offer_constraints.shape: outcome (quantified) + time (specific) + risk-mitigation (guarantee / free / no strings)

cta_constraints.must_propose: two specific times AND a fallback path (reply-with-time or cal.com link)

A.positioning: AI feature stuck in your eng backlog for months — I ship it in weeks, fixed-price, not another FTE hire
opener_examples: Saw {company} just raised — is the ai feature on your roadmap still sitting in the backlog?
Noticed you posted about shipping {topic} — is the ai piece live yet or still on the backlog?
{company} looks like it's in prod already — is there an ai feature you keep pushing to next sprint?
Looks like {company} is hiring — is the ai piece something you'd hire senior or outsource fixed-price?
give_first_examples: Spotted your careers page still lists a senior ai eng role open — that's the exact gap I fill without the FTE overhead.
Looked at {company}'s public roadmap — the {feature} piece is exactly what I've shipped 3 times this year at similar seed-stage cos.
Watched your last demo — the ai suggestion feature would be a 2-week build, not a quarter.
Read your latest LinkedIn post on {topic} — the ai feature you sketched is 10 days of work, not two months.
who_am_i_examples: I've shipped 12 ai systems end-to-end this year — cv-optimizer (live saas), a job-search v2, three internal tools — all 30 days or less, fixed-price.
15 years in data + ai product. Shipped 12 live ai systems this year, median 30 days zero-to-live. Currently running an outbound engine doing $1m modeled pipeline.
Last 12 months: 12 ai systems shipped, 100+ hours/week of client ops automated, one two-sided marketplace ($85k processed in 3 months).
Senior ai builder — 15 years product, 12 systems live, median 30 days. Ex-Wiser Solutions (+45% adoption, -55% latency on their genai rollout).
offer_examples: Let me ship your ai feature — first prototype in 14 days, or you don't pay.
I'll build and ship your ai feature in 30 days for a fixed price, or you get every euro back.
First working version of your ai feature in 14 days for €5-15k fixed. If it doesn't ship, you don't owe me.
One ai build in 30 days, fixed-price. If it isn't in your users' hands live, no invoice.
cta_time_proposals: Free 30-min scoping call, no deck no pitch. 15:30 Paris time today or Tuesday 10:00 — reply which works, or grab a slot: {cal_com_url}
Can I ring you today around 15:30 Paris time or Wednesday 10:00? Or if easier, grab a slot: {cal_com_url}
Free scoping call whenever — 15:30 CET today, or 10:00 tomorrow? Or {cal_com_url} if you'd rather pick your own time.
Reply with a 30-min window today or tomorrow — or {cal_com_url} if calendar's easier.

B.positioning: the machine that books meetings while you sleep + hands your team back 100+ hours a week — I built one that does $1m modeled pipeline for a similar shop
opener_examples: Saw {company} on {source} — is your pipeline still sdr-driven or automated yet?
{company} looks lean — how much of your team's week goes to manual outbound right now?
Noticed you're hiring an sdr — is that a $60k hire or a fixed-price build decision?
Looked at {company} — is the client-ops piece still done by hand or automated yet?
give_first_examples: Ran a quick check on your site — spotted 3 gaps in the outbound funnel worth about $10-20k/mo, happy to send the writeup no strings.
Looked at your team page — the 5 people you have are worth way more on client work than sdr work. That's fixable in 30 days.
Saw your job ad for a senior sdr — the $60k+benefits+ramp cost is 3x what a machine that does the same work costs to build once.
Watched your case study video — the pipeline stage you flagged as 'weakest' is the exact one I fixed for a similar Paris agency last quarter.
who_am_i_examples: I built and ran an outbound engine doing 48,000 emails/mo at 4%+ reply for a Paris agency — $1m modeled pipeline, replaced ~$200k/yr of sdr cost.
Runs the outbound machine you'd need — 48k emails/mo, 4%+ reply, 32 warmed inboxes, 3-hour hot-lead sla. Replaced a $200k sdr team for one agency.
Built the same thing you're thinking about: 48k/mo cold-email engine, 32 inboxes, 4%+ reply, $1m pipeline modeled. Runs on autopilot.
15 years in product + ai. Ran a $200k-replacing outbound machine for a Paris agency last year. 100+ hours/week of client ops automated across live systems.
offer_examples: Let me build you the same outbound machine — live in 30 days, replaces your next sdr hire, fixed-price or you get every euro back.
First working outbound engine in 21 days for a fixed price — replaces one sdr hire minimum. If it doesn't book meetings, no invoice.
30-day build: outbound engine live, 800 emails/day, targeting your icp. Fixed price. If reply rate is under 2%, full refund.
One outbound build + one ops-automation build in 30 days, fixed-price. If they don't save you $10k/mo minimum, you owe nothing.
cta_time_proposals: Free 30-min call — no deck, no pitch, we scope on the call. 15:30 CET today or Tuesday 10:00? Or {cal_com_url}
Can I ring you today around 15:30 Paris time or Wednesday 10:00? Or {cal_com_url} if easier.
Free scoping call — 15:30 CET today, 10:00 tomorrow, or {cal_com_url} whenever suits.
Reply with a 30-min window this week — or {cal_com_url} if calendar's easier.

C.positioning: a senior operator who scopes AND builds — de-risks your roadmap because he can do both. Fractional, live in weeks, not FTE headcount.
opener_examples: Which ai initiative on the {company} roadmap keeps getting deprioritized right now?
Saw {company}'s recent launch — is the next ai feature already scoped or still exploring?
Looked at {company} — is the ai piece a fractional build or FTE debate right now?
Noticed {topic} in your product update — is the ai layer live yet or on the backlog?
give_first_examples: Read your last product update — the ai piece you mentioned is a scope + build I've done 3x this year at Series B cos, happy to share the playbook no strings.
Spotted the ai feature on your public roadmap — same shape as one I shipped in 21 days at a similar-size scaleup, can send the postmortem.
Looked at your changelog — the ai piece is exactly the sort of scope-and-build where a fractional beats FTE on time-to-live.
Saw your recent all-hands mention of the roadmap — I've helped 3 Heads of Product de-risk this exact shape this year.
who_am_i_examples: 15 years in data-heavy product. Shipped 12 ai systems this year end-to-end. Ex-Wiser Solutions: +45% adoption, -55% latency, +20% csat on their genai rollout.
Senior product + build. 12 live ai systems shipped this year, median 30 days. Ran the enterprise genai/rag rollout at Wiser (+45% adoption, -55% latency).
I both scope and build — 15 years in product, 12 ai systems shipped in the last year. Last enterprise rollout: +45% feature adoption at Wiser.
Fractional ai builder — I do both the scoping and the code. 12 systems shipped this year, ex-Wiser (+45% adoption on their genai rollout).
offer_examples: Let me de-risk one ai feature on your roadmap — first working version in 30 days, fixed-price. If it doesn't ship live, no invoice.
One ai feature scoped and shipped in 45 days for a fixed price. Live in production or full refund.
First working version of your top-priority ai feature in 30 days, fixed-price. If it doesn't ship, you owe nothing.
30-day fractional engagement — I scope and build one ai feature end to end. Fixed price. If it doesn't ship live, refund.
cta_time_proposals: Free 30-min scoping call — no deck, no pitch. 15:30 CET today or Tuesday 10:00? Or {cal_com_url}
Can I ring you today at 15:30 Paris time or tomorrow at 10:00? Or grab a slot: {cal_com_url}
Free scoping call — 15:30 CET today, 10:00 tomorrow, or {cal_com_url} whenever works.

cta.primary: Free 30-min scoping call — 15:30 CET today or tomorrow 10:00, reply which works. Or {cal_com_url} if calendar's easier. No deck, no pitch.

follow_up._doctrine: Start with 2-email sequence. Human ping, not case study. Rotate subject on F2.
follow_up.f2_template: Hey {first_name}, checking in on {topic}. TLDR — if this is the first time you're seeing this: {restated_body}. Thanks, Debanjan
f2_human_pings: Hey {first_name}, are you cool man? Get back to me when you can.
Hey {first_name}, just checking in on {topic}. Let me know.
Hey {first_name}, let me know if this got lost.
Hey {first_name}, quick ping on the ai piece we discussed.
follow_up.expansion_trigger: reply rate on 2-step over baseline before adding F3

signature._note: 15% of emails should have one deliberate small imperfection at the END — a lowercase word, an abbreviation, a missing period — per Nick's humanization doctrine. Never at the start.

email_length_target._note: Nick's rewrites in the source video average 90-130 words. Under 65 is too thin to establish trust. Over 130 loses attention past the offer.
